subject: Computer Networking
title: Tracking Pixels and Privacy
description: A fast breakdown of Tracking Pixels and Privacy for builders.

hook.text: Did they open your email? A tiny invisible image tells the sender exactly when and where you read it.

buildup.text: A tracking pixel is a 1×1 transparent image hosted on the sender's server. Loading it logs the open event.

discovery.text: The log captures your IP, device type, OS, and exact time. All from opening one email.

twist.text: Apple Mail Privacy Protection now pre-fetches all images. The sender sees an open even if you never read it.

climax.text: Hey.com blocks all trackers by default and shows which emails tried to spy on you. A privacy revolt.

punchline.text: Tracking pixels spy silently. Privacy tools are fighting back.

quiz.question: How does Apple Mail Privacy Protection defeat tracking pixels?
options: It pre-fetches all images so real open data is hidden
It blocks all emails containing images
It converts images to text descriptions
